title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Joyce sits in her attic room with her aging, twelve-year-old fox terrier, known by various names including Tris, Tricky, and Trixie. Her landlady, Mrs. Barnes, brings Joyce a cup of tea and mentions that she is unemployed again, contributing to the household's financial instability. Mrs. Barnes complains that the dog bit her earlier that morning while she was looking at Joyce's shoes, but Joyce defends the animal, stating it is merely protective of her belongings. After Mrs. Barnes leaves, Joyce expresses her deep anxiety to the dog.

Joyce reveals that she and the dog owe four weeks' rent to Mrs. Bunce. She expresses frustration with the local men, noting that Mr. Bunce is frequently unemployed due to his drinking. Reflecting on her past, Joyce remembers happy times with the dog, her father, and Michael. She recalls how Michael used to take them to the countryside and how the dog supported her when she received a telegram announcing Michael's death. Joyce discloses that Michael had gambled away his money, a fact she keeps secret from his aunts, who believe she is doing well.

Exhausted, hungry, and feeling much older than her twenty-nine years, Joyce admits she lacks the strength to beg for help. She mentions a failed attempt to visit her cousin, Charlotte Green, in Iling. Despite the dog's declining health and temperament, Joyce vows never to separate from the animal. The passage ends when Joyce hears two knocks on the door downstairs.
